58dcdee1fb75.title: Hate small talk? You may enjoy that ‘dull’ chat more than you think, say researchers
58dcdee1fb75.summary: Новое исследование показало, что люди получают удовольствие от общения, даже если тема разговора кажется им скучной. Ученые обнаружили, что люди больше наслаждаются "пресными" беседами, чем ожидалось, несмотря на общую склонность избегать скуки.
58dcdee1fb75.original_description:
Participants reported enjoying the human connection regardless of whether they thought the topic was dull

The human aversion to dull experiences was nailed by the author Paulo Coelho when he declared: “I can stand defeats, pain, anger. But I can’t stand boredom.”

But the natural desire to avoid boring conversations comes at a cost, according to researchers, who found that people enjoyed chatting about tedious topics far more than they expected.

With the climate crisis accelerating, Scott could feel and see the seasons changing from their usual patterns – especially summer – and he wanted to know what the data said.

efc38fabc9b7.title: “Giant superatoms” could finally solve quantum computing’s biggest problem
efc38fabc9b7.summary: Ученые из Chalmers University of Technology предложили новую теоретическую модель "гигантских суператомов", которая может решить ключевую проблему стабильности в квантовых вычислениях. Эта разработка открывает новые возможности для защиты, контроля и распределения квантовой информации, приближая создание масштабируемых квантовых компьютеров.
efc38fabc9b7.original_description: In the pursuit of powerful and stable quantum computers, researchers at Chalmers University of Technology, Sweden, have developed the theory for an entirely new quantum system – based on the novel concept of ‘giant superatoms’. This breakthrough enables quantum information to be protected, controlled, and distributed in new ways and could be a key step towards building quantum computers at scale.

910271c171f9.title: New toothpaste stops gum disease without killing good bacteria
910271c171f9.summary: Scientists have developed a new way to fight gum disease without wiping out the mouth’s helpful bacteria—a major shift from traditional treatments. Instead of killing everything, this targeted approach blocks only the harmful microbes that drive periodontitis, allowing beneficial bacteria to thrive and restore balance naturally.

9998ffeab8a9.title: Africa’s forests have flipped from carbon sink to carbon source
9998ffeab8a9.summary: Леса Африки изменили свой статус с поглотителей углерода на источники выбросов после 2010 года. Массивные вырубки в тропических регионах привели к значительной потере биомассы, превышающей прирост от восстановления лесов. Это изменение представляет серьезную угрозу глобальным усилиям по борьбе с изменением климата, подчеркивая необходимость срочной защиты африканских лесов.
9998ffeab8a9.original_description: Africa’s forests have undergone a shocking reversal, switching from carbon absorbers to carbon emitters after 2010. Researchers found that heavy deforestation in tropical regions has led to massive biomass losses, far outweighing any gains from regrowth elsewhere. This change could seriously undermine global efforts to slow climate change. Scientists warn that protecting forests is now more urgent than ever.

9e13702b4b93.title: Men and women with obesity face very different hidden health risks
9e13702b4b93.summary: New research reveals that obesity affects men and women in surprisingly different ways. Men are more likely to develop harmful abdominal fat and signs of liver stress, while women show higher inflammation and cholesterol levels. These differences could help explain why health risks vary between sexes. Scientists say this could lead to more tailored treatments for obesity.

2d6b6bfcde6e.title: Gray whales are entering San Francisco Bay and many aren’t surviving
2d6b6bfcde6e.summary: Gray whales are beginning to break their long-established migration patterns, venturing into risky new territory like San Francisco Bay as climate change disrupts their Arctic food supply. But this unexpected detour is proving deadly: nearly one in five whales that enter the Bay don’t survive, with many struck by ships in the crowded, foggy waters.

19bb21cb1518.title: Light makes plants stronger but also holds them back
19bb21cb1518.summary: Light doesn’t just help plants grow—it may also quietly hold them back. Researchers have uncovered a surprising mechanism where light strengthens the “glue” between a plant’s outer skin and its inner tissues. This tighter bond, driven by a compound called p-coumaric acid, reinforces cell walls but also restricts how much the plant can expand. The discovery reveals a hidden balancing act: light both fuels growth and subtly puts the brakes on it.

Данное соединение, выявленное с помощью искусственного интеллекта, имитирует эффект лекарств, таких как Оземиптик, но без значительных побочных эффектов, таких как тошнота и потеря мышечной массы. Исследования на животных показали, что BRP способствует снижению аппетита и потере жира.
232524c3dca3.original_description: A newly discovered molecule could reshape the future of weight loss treatments by mimicking the powerful appetite-suppressing effects of drugs like Ozempic — but without many of the unpleasant side effects. Identified using artificial intelligence, this tiny peptide, called BRP, appears to act directly on the brain’s appetite-control center, helping animals eat less and lose fat without nausea or muscle loss.

3c0d1c54d2b9.title: Two simple eating habits linked to lower weight, study finds
3c0d1c54d2b9.summary: A major study suggests that when you eat could play a key role in staying lean. People who fast longer overnight and start their day with an early breakfast were more likely to have a lower BMI years later. Scientists think this is because eating earlier aligns better with the body’s internal clock. But skipping breakfast as part of intermittent fasting didn’t offer the same advantage—and may even be tied to unhealthy habits.

2f186a8b202a.title: A 67-year-old “crazy” theory about vitamin B1 has finally been proven
2f186a8b202a.summary: Scientists have achieved the unthinkable by stabilizing a highly reactive molecule in water, confirming a decades-old theory about vitamin B1’s role in the body. The breakthrough not only solves a scientific mystery but could revolutionize greener chemical manufacturing.

e9382d42c2a8.title: Your nose could detect Alzheimer’s years before symptoms begin
e9382d42c2a8.summary: Losing your sense of smell might signal Alzheimer’s far earlier than expected. Scientists found that immune cells in the brain actively destroy smell-related nerve fibers after detecting abnormal signals on their surfaces. This damage begins in early stages of the disease, well before cognitive decline. The discovery could help identify at-risk patients sooner and improve treatment timing.

806401318a7b.title: Scientists were wrong about lifespan. Your genes matter way more than we thought
806401318a7b.summary: Новое исследование Мысского института переворачивает представления о продолжительности жизни. Ученые обнаружили, что генетика играет гораздо более значимую роль, чем считалось ранее, определяя около половины различий в продолжительности жизни людей. Исследование, основанное на анализе больших парных сестёр, показало, что генетические факторы оказывают существенное влияние на то, как долго мы живем.
806401318a7b.original_description: For years, scientists believed our lifespan was mostly shaped by environment and chance, with genetics playing only a minor role. But a new study from the Weizmann Institute flips that idea on its head, revealing that genes may actually account for about half of the differences in how long people live. By analyzing massive twin datasets—including twins raised apart—and using innovative simulations to filter out deaths from accidents and other external causes, researchers uncovered a hidden genet

0d04480000c5.title: Neanderthals may have hunted and eaten outsiders, chilling cannibalism study finds
0d04480000c5.summary: Новое исследование, основанное на находках в бельгийской пещере, свидетельствует о том, что неандертальцы охотились и поедали представителей других групп, особенно женщин и детей.  Судя по всему, это было не ритуальное каннибализм, а практическое использование добычи, возможно, связанное с межгрупповыми конфликтами, что добавляет сложности в понимание жизни неандертальцев.
0d04480000c5.original_description: A cave in Belgium has revealed unsettling evidence that Neanderthals selectively cannibalized outsiders, focusing on women and children. The victims weren’t from the local group and appear to have been treated like prey, with bones butchered for meat and marrow. This suggests the behavior wasn’t ritual, but practical—or possibly linked to intergroup conflict. The discovery paints a darker, more complex picture of Neandertal life during their final millennia.

63533e0c3e7d.title: Black hole wakes after 100 million years and erupts like a cosmic volcano
63533e0c3e7d.summary: В галактике J1007+3540 супермассивная черная дыра, не проявлявшая активности около 100 миллионов лет, вновь активизировалась. Астрономы зафиксировали мощные радиоизлучения и выбросы плазмы, образующие огромную структуру протяженностью в миллион световых лет, взаимодействующую с близлежащим скоплением галактик.
63533e0c3e7d.original_description: A colossal “cosmic volcano” has erupted in deep space, as a supermassive black hole in galaxy J1007+3540 roars back to life after nearly 100 million years of silence. Astronomers captured stunning radio images showing fresh jets blasting outward while crashing into the intense pressure of a surrounding galaxy cluster, creating a chaotic, distorted structure stretching nearly a million light-years.

770bc39db659.title: Forget daily pills. This shot works when blood pressure meds fail
770bc39db659.summary: A twice-yearly injection may soon change how high blood pressure is treated. In a global trial, patients receiving the experimental drug zilebesiran alongside standard therapy saw greater blood pressure reductions than those on standard treatment alone. The drug works by blocking a key liver protein, helping blood vessels relax. Researchers say this long-lasting approach could make it much easier for patients to keep their condition under control.

db5dfafdd289.title: Life on Mars? Tiny cells just survived shock waves and toxic soil
db5dfafdd289.summary: Mars may be hostile, but it might not be entirely unlivable. In lab experiments, yeast cells survived simulated Martian shock waves and toxic perchlorate salts—two major environmental threats on the Red Planet. Their secret weapon was forming protective molecular clusters that shield critical cellular functions under stress. Without these defenses, survival plummeted, pointing to a potential universal strategy life could use beyond Earth.

55503eadf49c.title: How aggressive breast cancer turns off the immune system
55503eadf49c.summary: Researchers are launching a new project to crack the mystery of aggressive breast cancer, where predicting disease progression remains a major hurdle. By studying how tumors interact with and suppress the immune system, scientists aim to identify new biomarkers that reveal how the cancer evolves. Using real patient samples, the team hopes to turn earlier discoveries into practical clinical tools. The goal: more precise, personalized treatments that can outsmart even the most dangerous tumors.

c7f8bfd3a056.title: Why Ozempic doesn’t work for everyone: Scientists just found a hidden reason
c7f8bfd3a056.summary: A new study reveals that popular diabetes and weight-loss drugs like Ozempic and Wegovy may not work as effectively for about 10% of people due to specific genetic variants. These individuals appear to have a puzzling condition called “GLP-1 resistance,” where their bodies produce higher levels of the hormone targeted by these drugs—but don’t respond to it properly.

fbe005da1c24.title: Hidden weak spots in HIV and Ebola revealed with breakthrough nanodisc technology

Это привело к обнаружению ранее неизвестных взаимодействий вирусных белков, что может ускорить разработку более эффективных вакцин и улучшить понимание работы иммунной системы.
fbe005da1c24.original_description: A new nanodisc-based platform lets scientists study viral proteins in a form that closely mimics real viruses, revealing how antibodies truly recognize them. This approach uncovered hidden interactions in viruses like HIV and Ebola that traditional methods missed. By recreating the virus’s membrane environment, researchers can better understand how immune defenses work. The technique could speed up the development of more effective vaccines.

77c46f628f81.title: Ken Loach revisits I, Daniel Blake: ‘We were asking if food banks are tolerable. Now they’re an institution’
77c46f628f81.summary:
The scene at the food bank, recalls the director, where Katie is so hungry, she pours baked beans into her hand from a tin and eats them cold, came from a real story

In 2016, we were – as we continue to be – in a time of mean-spiritedness. If you were vulnerable or needed support, you were met with punishment, and there was a constant vilifying of people who needed help. I, Daniel Blake was based on that.
